title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Object Reference

statement.markdown: Create a React memoized component that displays a greeting based on the user's name. The component should only re-render when the name changes. However, the current implementation is causing unnecessary re-renders due to changing object references. Fix the code to optimize the re-renders.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an object with a single property `name` of type string.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a string greeting the user by their name.
statement.notes: The component should only re-render when the name property changes. Use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the greeting.

constraints.description: Number of times the component is rendered

examples.explanation: The component should only re-render when the name property changes.
The component should only re-render when the name property changes.

hints.text: Use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the greeting.

editorial.title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders
editorial.intuition: The `useMemo` hook can be used to memoize the greeting, so that it only re-renders when the name property changes.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to re-render the component on every state change, but this would cause unnecessary re-renders.
editorial.betterSolution: Use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the greeting and only re-render the component when the name property changes.
editorial.optimalSolution: Create a memoized component that uses the `useMemo` hook to memoize the greeting, and only re-renders when the name property changes.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The component should only re-render when the name property changes, which is guaranteed by the `useMemo` hook.
commonMistakes: Not checking for object reference changes
